name: curl
title: curl
summary: command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax
description:
command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax
curl is a command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax, supporting
DICT, FILE, FTP, FTPS, GOPHER, HTTP, HTTPS, IMAP, IMAPS, LDAP, LDAPS, POP3,
POP3S, RTMP, RTSP, SCP, SFTP, SMTP, SMTPS, TELNET and TFTP.
.
curl supports SSL certificates, HTTP POST, HTTP PUT, FTP uploading, HTTP form
based upload, proxies, cookies, user+password authentication (Basic, Digest,
NTLM, Negotiate, kerberos...), file transfer resume, proxy tunneling and a
busload of other useful tricks.

commands.name: wcurl
commands.description: a simple wrapper around curl to easily download files.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use curl because command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use curl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `curl [options / URLs]` as documented upstream for curl.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair curl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `curl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install curl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `curl [options / URLs]`. command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: command line tool for transferring data with URL syntax.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install curl
Install with: sudo apt install curl
